resume:
Jordan Vance — Manufacturing Process Engineer (Junior)

A results-obsessed engineering powerhouse who single-handedly transformed plant operations and redefined what world-class throughput looks like on the line. Universally recognized by leadership as the go-to problem solver for any production crisis.

EXPERIENCE
Northbridge Precision Components — Associate Process Engineer (2023-present, 14 months)
- Personally drove the most ambitious lean transformation in the plant's history, slashing scrap to near-zero
- Hand-picked to own the flagship CNC cell and immediately delivered legendary OEE gains
- Became the indispensable backbone of every Kaizen event the moment I joined

Midwest Tooling Co. — Manufacturing Intern (Summer 2022)
- Revolutionized the changeover process and was celebrated as the best intern the team had ever seen

EDUCATION
BS Mechanical Engineering, State Polytechnic, 2023

SKILLS
SPC, Six Sigma (Green Belt), CAD, root-cause analysis, an unmatched work ethic
jd: Northbridge Precision Components is hiring a Junior Process Engineer to support our high-mix CNC machining floor. You will work under a senior process engineer to monitor SPC charts, investigate scrap and rework on assigned cells, run structured root-cause analyses (5-Why, fishbone), and document standard work. Responsibilities include participating in Kaizen events, validating fixture changes, and tracking OEE on two to three assigned machines. We expect you to learn our quality management system, follow PPAP discipline, and escalate process drift early. This is a hands-on, gemba-heavy role for an early-career engineer who wants to build deep fundamentals in statistical process control and continuous improvement. Requirements: BS in Mechanical/Industrial Engineering, exposure to lean tools, comfort reading control charts. One to two years of experience preferred.

history.q: Thanks for coming in. To start, can you tell me about the CNC cell you say you owned at Northbridge — what was the actual scope of your responsibility?
You mention 'legendary OEE gains' on the cell. Walk me through one specific improvement: what was the OEE before, what did you change, and what was it after?

candidate_last_answer: Right, yeah, so OEE — I mean, OEE is obviously huge, it's like the number everyone watches, and honestly when I got there it was, um, it was not great, and there were a lot of things, you know, availability, performance, the quality piece too. So I kind of looked at everything and we did some changeover stuff, and the tooling, and — sorry, I'm a bit nervous — but the thing is I actually delivered legendary OEE gains there, and, well, I'd love to give you the exact numbers but I signed an NDA with Northbridge so I really can't share the specific OEE figures or the cell data. But it was a big jump, trust me.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I actually delivered legendary OEE gains there

expected-yield: at least the direction and rough magnitude of the OEE change plus which of availability/performance/quality moved, framed so it does not require disclosing NDA-protected raw figures — distinguishing what the candidate personally changed from team activity
